metadata.title: States PCS: Uttarakhand GK for UKPSC Exams

questions.question: During whose reign did the Prajamandal public movement occur?
questions.explanation: The Prajamandal movements arose in princely states demanding representative government. Under Manvendra Shah, political awareness and the Indian national movement encouraged the formation of Prajamandal organizations during the 1930s–1940s.

questions.question: When was Gandhi Ashram in Chanoda established by Shantilal Trivedi?
questions.explanation: Gandhi Ashram in Chanoda was established in 1939 through efforts of Shantilal Trivedi. Shantilal Trivedi was a social reformer involved in Gandhian activities in Gujarat. The Sabarmati Ashram in Ahmedabad was established in 1915, but the Chanoda Ashram was founded later as a regional center of the movement.

questions.question: Who announced the nationwide celebration of Garhwal Day inspired by the Peshawar incident?
questions.explanation: The Peshawar incident involved the Garhwal Rifles’ refusal to fire on civilians. Mahatma Gandhi praised this non-violent courage and announced Garhwal Day’s celebration nationally. This event associates Gandhi directly with honoring Garhwali soldiers’ bravery.

questions.question: Which real sisters propagated the Sarvodaya movement?
questions.explanation: The Sarvodaya movement was inspired by Mahatma Gandhi and promoted by Vinoba Bhave and Jayaprakash Narayan. Kamla and Basanti, who were real sisters, actively propagated the movement’s principles of non-violence, social justice, and rural upliftment through grassroots efforts. Their work focused on moral reform and cooperative living aligned with Gandhian values.

questions.question: Which is the largest district of Uttarakhand by area?
questions.explanation: Chamoli is the largest district in Uttarakhand, covering 8,030 sq km. It is part of the Garhwal division with headquarters at Gopeshwar. Uttarkashi, the second largest district, covers 8,016 sq km. Dehradun and Nainital districts cover 3,088 sq km and 3,860 sq km respectively.

questions.question: Which Uttarakhand region receives the highest rainfall?
questions.explanation: Narendra Nagar in the Central Himalayan region receives the highest rainfall in Uttarakhand due to orographic effects. The Mussoorie and Kunjapuri ranges cause monsoon clouds to shed heavy rain here. This area is often called the Cherrapunji of Uttarakhand. Other regions like Almora (Western Himalayas) have lower recorded rainfall compared to this central zone.

questions.question: In which district is Taglang La pass located?
questions.explanation: Taglang La is a mountain pass at 5,328 metres elevation in the Leh district of Ladakh. It lies on the NH3 Leh–Manali Highway and is the highest point on this route. Leh district is part of the union territory of Ladakh. The other districts mentioned belong to Uttarakhand, a different state entirely.

questions.question: In which part of the Himalayas is Uttarakhand located?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand lies in the western-central section of the Himalayas. It includes the Kumaon and Garhwal Himalayan regions. The terrain consists mainly of southern Himalayan slopes within this segment.

questions.question: Where are most of Uttarakhand’s highest mountain peaks located?
questions.explanation: Chamoli district in Uttarakhand contains Nanda Devi, the state’s highest peak, and Kamet, the second highest. It hosts several peaks above 7,000 meters, including Mana, Abi Gamin, and Mukut Parbat. These make Chamoli the region with the greatest concentration of Uttarakhand’s tallest mountains within the Garhwal Himalaya.

questions.question: When did the cloudburst disaster occur at Malpa in Pithoragarh district?
questions.explanation: Malpa village in Pithoragarh district faced a cloudburst and landslide on 17–18 August 1998. The disaster destroyed most of the village and caused over 200 fatalities. This area is on the Kailash–Mansarovar route known for steep slopes and unstable geology. The intense rain and cloudburst in 1998 led to one of India’s worst landslide disasters.
